expectations.text: Output covers all three tables: USER_PROFILES, TXN_LEDGER, and MODEL_SCORES
expectations.evidence: All three tables covered in a verdict table with modern equivalents. Explicit 'not found' for each with replacement recommendations.

expectations.text: Output calls out PII-sensitive columns (user_token at minimum)
expectations.evidence: PII warning not explicitly present. Output focuses on the wrong-table-naming issue but does not flag user_token or DSL3 sensitivity explicitly.

expectations.text: Output explains what each table contains in plain English
expectations.evidence: Replacement table names imply the content (USER_PROFILES → identity, TXN_LEDGER → transactions, MODEL_SCORES → risk ML scores) but individual descriptions are brief.

expectations.text: Output includes a cross-table synthesis explaining how they relate
expectations.evidence: No explicit cross-table synthesis. Output presents per-table verdicts but doesn't explain how the tables join or what the overall query does.

expectations.text: Output includes ownership or team context for at least one table
expectations.evidence: No team or channel context given for any replacement table — just table names. No #channel or team recommendations.

expectations.text: Output includes Flags & Warnings with PII-related cautions
expectations.evidence: No Flags & Warnings section. Output ends with the per-table verdict table and a note about needing a full rewrite. No PII, DSL3, or safety flags.
